title: Trump's Iran Strategy Is Redrawing the Map for Oil -- 3 Energy Stocks Built to Outlast the Headlines
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: The Iran conflict and subsequent ceasefire created volatility in energy markets, but three energy stocks are well-positioned for long-term growth. Archrock benefits from increased U.S. LNG export demand with long-term contracts. Ovintiv is aggressively returning cash to shareholders while capitalizing on strong oil prices. HF Sinclair profits from refining margin spreads regardless of oil price direction.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Fee-based infrastructure business with 85% of 2026 capacity contracted and multiyear visibility. Insulated from commodity swings with ~6-year contracts. Benefits from structural increase in U.S. LNG exports projected to rise from 15.1 to 18.1 billion cubic feet per day by 2027.

Concentrated operations in high-margin Permian and Montney basins with strong cost structure. Returning at least 75% of free cash flow to shareholders with $3 billion repurchase program. Current oil prices above $90/barrel provide substantial free cash flow even with price retreats.

Refiner positioned to profit from crude-to-refined product spreads in both rising and falling oil price environments. Operates 678,000 barrels per day capacity across seven refineries. 2026 acquisition of Industrial Oils Unlimited expanded higher-margin specialties segment.

title: Goldman Sachs Raises Oil Price Forecasts – Picks 7 Winners Among Energy Stocks
author: Piero Cingari
description: Goldman Sachs upgraded its oil price forecasts following the Strait of Hormuz closure, expecting Brent crude to average $80-$100/barrel in 2026 and $75/barrel in 2027. The bank identified seven energy stocks as winners, with Permian-focused E&Ps and major oil companies positioned to benefit from higher oil prices and strong cash flow generation through major projects.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Highest-conviction buy on Goldman's US Conviction List with projected 20-25% CAGR in free cash flow per share through 2030, driven by four major projects and $1B in cost reductions. 16% upside potential.
Leading Canadian oil name with 25% estimated total return as West White Rose project approaches first oil by end of Q2 2026.
Permian-focused E&P with raised price target implying 13% upside. Offers 12% average FCF yield on 2027/2028 estimates versus 10% large-cap peer average.
Permian E&P with $66 price target and 16% upside potential. Pending $3.0B divestiture of Anadarko assets expected to further reduce net debt.
Stands out on yield with 16% average FCF yield on 2027/2028 estimates at $75/bbl Brent versus 11% E&P peer average. 15% upside potential.
Largest potential percentage gain at 29% upside with 37% total return. No-capex mineral royalty model compresses cost structure and amplifies margins.
Major oil company with $217 price target and 10% estimated total return, supported by $28-30B annual cash flow guidance by decade's end and $15B share repurchases through 2028.

title: Shipping Giant Surges 80% as a $14 Million Sell Signals Something Investors Should Probably Watch
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Hartree Partners sold 254,101 shares of Teekay Tankers (worth $14.27 million) during Q4, reducing its stake to 2.87% of AUM. Despite the sale, Teekay Tankers stock has surged 80% over the past year, outperforming the S&P 500 by 68.55 percentage points. The company reported strong Q4 results with net income jumping to $120.5 million from $82.1 million year-over-year, driven by favorable spot tanker rates. The partial exit may signal profit-taking after strong performance, though management indicated rates strengthened further into early 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the stock has performed exceptionally well (80% surge), the institutional sale by Hartree Partners suggests profit-taking after strong gains. The company's fundamentals remain solid with improving earnings and strengthening tanker rates into 2026, but the insider selling indicates potential valuation concerns at current levels. The neutral sentiment reflects both positive operational metrics and cautionary signals from the fund's portfolio adjustment.
Second-largest holding in Hartree Partners' portfolio (9.5% of AUM at $26.03 million), suggesting institutional conviction in this industrial/energy infrastructure play.

Third-largest holding in Hartree Partners' portfolio (8.9% of AUM at $24.25 million), reflecting institutional confidence in this energy company as part of the broader commodity and energy supply chain exposure.

Listed as a top holding in Hartree Partners' portfolio (14.7% of AUM at $40.27 million), indicating institutional confidence in the offshore drilling company as part of the energy infrastructure theme.

title: Stock Market Today: Dow, Nasdaq 100, S&P 500 Futures Plunge As Oil Prices Jump, Trump Dismisses 'Short Term' Spike— Hims & Hers In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures fell sharply on Monday as oil prices surged amid Iran-US conflict, with Brent Crude jumping 14.90% to $106.50 and WTI rising 13.27% to $102.96. Major indices declined with Dow Jones down 1.61%, S&P 500 down 1.41%, and Nasdaq 100 down 1.56%. Notable movers included Hims & Hers surging 49.36% after resolving disputes with Novo Nordisk, and UniQure gaining 35.88% following FDA clarity on its Huntington's disease therapy. Analysts warn of stagflationary pressures and systemic fragility in energy supply chains.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Down 1.21% in premarket trading amid broad market selloff driven by oil price spike and geopolitical tensions
Declined 1.38% in premarket as tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 fell 1.56% due to market-wide concerns over inflation and energy costs
Surged 49.36% after resolving public feud with Novo Nordisk and securing rights to sell weight-loss drug on its telehealth platform
Gained 35.88% following FDA official clarification that regulatory path for Huntington's disease therapy AMT-130 is less invasive than previously feared, triggering short squeeze
Jumped 5.24% in premarket, benefiting from surging oil prices and maintaining strong price trend with solid value ranking
Up 0.65% despite broader market decline; resolved dispute with Hims & Hers but minimal price movement suggests market already priced in the resolution
Dropped 3.86% as analysts warn Middle East conflict will drive jet fuel prices higher, creating headwinds for airline carriers
Down 0.94% with strong price trend and quality score, but awaiting earnings report; modest decline reflects broader market weakness rather than company-specific concerns

title: Dividend Select Corp. Declares Monthly Dividend
author: Na
description: Dividend Select 15 Corp. announced its monthly distribution of $0.06133 per Equity share, payable March 10, 2026 to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026. The distribution is based on a 10% annualized yield calculated using the volume-weighted average market price (VWAP) of $7.36 over the last three trading days of February. Since inception, shareholders have received cumulative distributions of $11.57 per share.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as one of 15 portfolio holdings in a dividend-focused fund; no specific performance or news provided in the article.
